hero.hero_title_1:
Run Any Database Natively on
Microsoft AKS -
Without the Compromise.
hero.hero_description: Deploy and manage 25+ Production-grade databases as native Kubernetes resources on Microsoft AKS. Leverage Azure infrastructure with broader choice, zero extra licensing, and true GitOps agility.

paragraph.content: KubeDB by AppsCode delivers a production-grade, Kubernetes-native Database-as-a-Service platform that runs seamlessly on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). It enables platform teams and developers to provision, manage, scale, backup, and recover a wide range of databases using familiar Kubernetes APIs and declarative workflows - without vendor lock-in or reliance on Azure-managed database services.

what_this_means.heading: What This Means for Azure and AKS Users
what_this_means.description: If you're running applications on Azure AKS, you already enjoy scalable, managed Kubernetes. However, databases often introduce complexity through separate tooling, manual operations, or cloud-specific DBaaS services that create lock-in.
what_this_means.subtitle: KubeDB changes that by making databases first-class Kubernetes resources directly inside your AKS clusters:

items.title: Faster Time-to-Value
items.description: Launch databases in minutes using kubectl, Helm, GitOps, or self-service portals.

items.title: Lower Operational Burden
items.description: Automate Day-2 tasks and eliminate custom scripts.

items.title: Cost Optimization
items.description: Use existing Azure storage and avoid premium managed DBaaS pricing.

items.title: Hybrid/Multi-Cloud Consistency
items.description: Same workflows whether running on AKS or other clouds.

architecture.heading: KubeDB runs natively on Microsoft AKS
architecture.description: The KubeDB Operator sits directly on top of your AKS clusters, leveraging Azure infrastructure, availability zone capabilities, and Kubernetes-native patterns for database automation.

layers.label: KubeDB Platform
layers.desc: Web Console • Envoy Gateway • Hub/Spoke Model • Policy Engine

layers.label: Kubernetes Services
layers.desc: ArgoCD • Open Cluster Management • Cert Manager • Prometheus • Grafana

layers.label: Microsoft AKS
layers.desc: Kubernetes • Azure Infrastructure • Availability Zone Support

architecture.image_alt: KubeDB Platform Architecture on Microsoft AKS

capabilities.heading: Production databases. Fully automated on AKS.
capabilities.description: 25+ Database engines with HA, scaling, backup, TLS, monitoring, and GitOps support

items.title: Run Databases Natively on AKS
items.description: KubeDB integrates deeply with AKS, supporting any Kubernetes StorageClass (including Azure Disks, Azure Files, or third-party CSI drivers). Databases run inside your private pod network with full affinity and anti-affinity controls for availability zone resiliency.

items.title: DBaaS Without Cloud Lock-In
items.description: Complete data ownership and sovereignty with portability across clouds or on-prem. Support for air-gapped and sovereign environments with your choice of storage optimized for performance, cost, or resilience.

items.title: Broad Database Support
items.description: Manage 25+ Database engines including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, Kafka, and more, all with consistent Kubernetes-native workflows.

items.title: Automated Lifecycle Management
items.description: Declarative CRDs handle provisioning, upgrades, scaling and repair with zero-downtime where possible.

items.title: High Availability & Resiliency
items.description: Multi-zone clustering, automated failover, PodDisruptionBudgets, and continuous health checks.

items.title: Backup & Recovery
items.description: Integrated with KubeStash for policy-driven, incremental, encrypted backups to Azure Blob Storage. Support for point-in-time recovery.

items.title: Monitoring & Observability
items.description: Native Prometheus + Grafana dashboards. Integrates with Azure Monitor and Application Insights.

items.title: Security & Governance
items.description: Automated TLS, Transparent Data Encryption, RBAC integration with Azure AD, network policies, and Vault support.

items.title: Self-Service for Developers
items.description: Platform teams enforce policies while developers provision databases via Kubernetes-native workflows.

database_support.heading: Comprehensive Database Support
database_support.description: 25+ Database engines managed with Kubernetes-native workflows

categories.label: Relational
categories.items: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, Percona XtraDB

aws_integration.heading: Seamless Azure & Kubernetes Integration
aws_integration.description: KubeDB leverages the full power of Azure and Kubernetes to deliver a complete database platform

integrations.title: Azure AKS & Kubernetes
integrations.description: Deploy databases natively on Azure AKS with support for availability zones, Azure CNI, and Azure Policy. Full GitOps workflows with Flux.

integrations.title: Azure Blob Storage
integrations.description: Automated backup and archival to Azure Blob Storage with lifecycle policies, versioning, geo-replication, and hot/cool/archive tiers.

integrations.title: Azure Monitor & Application Insights
integrations.description: Deep integration with Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and native Prometheus support with pre-built Grafana dashboards.

integrations.title: Azure AD & Managed Identities
integrations.description: Use Azure Active Directory for authentication and Azure Managed Identities for secure credential management without secrets.

integrations.title: Azure Virtual Networks & Network Security
integrations.description: Deploy databases in your VNet with custom network configurations, NSGs, and private endpoints. Full Kubernetes NetworkPolicy support.

comparison.heading: Why KubeDB on AKS Beats Azure Database Services
comparison.description: Feature-by-feature, KubeDB on AKS delivers broader coverage, lower cost, and true portability.

items.title: Azure Key Vault
items.description: Encryption key management

items.title: Azure Disks
items.description: Premium SSD storage

items.title: Azure DNS
items.description: DNS management

items.title: ARM Templates
items.description: Infrastructure as code

items.title: Azure Policy
items.description: Governance & compliance

items.title: VMSS Autoscaling
items.description: Dynamic scaling

items.title: Azure Arc
items.description: Hybrid cloud management

items.title: Azure Files
items.description: Shared file storage

cost_comparison.heading: Why KubeDB Is More Cost-Effective Than Azure Database Services
cost_comparison.description: Run databases on Azure at a fraction of the cost while improving performance and developer productivity

savings_cards.title: Better Bin Packing
savings_cards.description: Run multiple databases on the same Azure VM, unlike Azure Database Services which requires dedicated instances

savings_cards.title: Spot VM Support
savings_cards.description: Use Azure Spot VMs for database pods, reducing compute costs by up to 90%

savings_cards.title: Lower Management Fees
savings_cards.description: Azure Database Services charges premium management fees on top of VM costs. KubeDB fees are significantly lower

savings_cards.title: Free for Short-Lived Workloads
savings_cards.description: Use KubeDB free for any clusters running less than 30 days (dev, test, CI/CD environments)

savings_cta.heading: Calculate Your Savings
savings_cta.description: Organizations typically save 60-70% on database costs when switching from Azure Database Services to KubeDB

cta.heading: Run Databases Natively
cta.description: 60% cost savings, 10x faster provisioning, zero vendor lock-in

features.text: Full Azure integration (Blob, AD, Key Vault)
